failure_modes.cause: High viscosity of TBAB/EG mixture at lower temperatures or salt precipitation during reaction
failure_modes.mitigation: Implement heat-tracing on all feed lines and a pre-heating coil before the reactor

failure_modes.cause: Local hotspots or prolonged exposure to 120°C causing tubing softening/deformation
failure_modes.mitigation: Use stainless steel or Hastelloy tubing for the high-temperature zone

failure_modes.cause: X=0.12 indicates 88% unreacted precursors entering the quench stage
failure_modes.mitigation: Increase residence time (L) or temperature to ensure conversion before quenching

single_points_of_failure: BPR valve — blockage due to DES viscosity/precipitation leads to immediate overpressure
validation_experiments: Long-term stability run (4 hours) at 120°C to monitor for pressure creep indicating fouling
Solubility test of reaction products in TBAB/EG at 20°C to assess clogging risk during shutdown
Conversion mapping: vary tau from 2.0 to 12.0 min to determine the minimum tau for safe quenching
